req.title: Biller registration (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-048 mandates a 24-hour hold after biller registration and defers withdrawals during that period. Four tests cover the critical paths: (1) EP positive – a new valid biller lands in HOLD state with HTTP 201; (2) NEG negative – a duplicate biller registration returns HTTP 409; (3) BVA boundary – a biller registered at exactly 24 hours (86400 s) minus one second is still in HOLD, while one at exactly 86400 s transitions to ACTIVE, probing the exact threshold; (4) STC state-transition – the full lifecycle NEW→HOLD→ACTIVE is traced. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up billers, and uses time injection via the API's test-clock header to remain deterministic.

tests.name: test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Biller with the target account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: POST /billers with valid biller payload (name, account_number, routing_number)
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains status field equal to 'HOLD'
Response body contains a hold_expires_at timestamp approximately 24 hours in the future
trace: R-FIN-048: customer adds biller → biller in hold state
R-FIN-048: 24-hour hold requirement – new biller must start in HOLD
tests.executable:
def test_register_new_biller_enters_hold_state(auth_headers, unique_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    resp = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=unique_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    biller_id = body["id"]
    cleanup_biller(biller_id)
    assert body["status"] == "HOLD"
    hold_expires = <parse_iso_datetime>(body["hold_expires_at"])
    now = <utcnow>()
    delta_hours = (hold_expires - now).total_seconds() / 3600
    assert 23.9 <= delta_hours <= 24.1, f"hold window out of range: {delta_hours}h"
    get_resp = <api_client>.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=auth_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "HOLD"

tests.name: test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A biller with the same account_number already exists for this customer
actions: POST /billers with biller payload for a pre-existing biller account_number
Capture HTTP response status and error body
expected: No new biller record is created
trace: R-FIN-048: error pathway – 409 if duplicate biller
tests.executable:
def test_register_duplicate_biller_returns_409(auth_headers, existing_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    first = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=existing_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201
    biller_id = first.json()["id"]
    cleanup_biller(biller_id)
    duplicate = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=existing_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert duplicate.status_code == 409
    err = duplicate.json()
    assert err.get("error_code") == "DUPLICATE_BILLER"
    list_resp = <api_client>.get("/billers", headers=auth_headers)
    billers = list_resp.json()["items"]
    matching = [b for b in billers if b["account_number"] == existing_biller_payload["account_number"]]
    assert len(matching) == 1, "Duplicate biller must not be persisted"

tests.name: test_biller_hold_boundary_at_exactly_24_hours
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
API supports X-Test-Clock header to override server time
Two unique biller payloads are available (one for 86399 s, one for 86400 s)
actions: POST /billers for biller_A; advance test clock to T+86399 s (one second before threshold); GET /billers/{id_A}
POST /billers for biller_B; advance test clock to T+86400 s (exact threshold); GET /billers/{id_B}
expected: At T+86399 s biller_A status is still 'HOLD'
At T+86400 s biller_B status is 'ACTIVE'
Withdrawal attempt on biller_A at T+86399 s is deferred (HTTP 422 or equivalent)
Withdrawal attempt on biller_B at T+86400 s is accepted (HTTP 2xx)
trace: R-FIN-048: boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested
R-FIN-048: withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred during hold
tests.executable:
def test_biller_hold_boundary_at_exactly_24_hours(auth_headers, unique_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    payload_a = {**unique_biller_payload, "account_number": <unique_account_number>()}
    payload_b = {**unique_biller_payload, "account_number": <unique_account_number>()}
    reg_a = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=payload_a, headers=auth_headers)
    assert reg_a.status_code == 201
    id_a = reg_a.json()["id"]
    cleanup_biller(id_a)
    headers_a = {**auth_headers, "X-Test-Clock": str(<utcnow_epoch>() + 86399)}
    status_a = <api_client>.get(f"/billers/{id_a}", headers=headers_a).json()["status"]
    assert status_a == "HOLD", f"Expected HOLD at T+86399, got {status_a}"
    wd_a = <api_client>.post("/withdrawals", json={"biller_id": id_a, "amount": "10.00"}, headers=headers_a)
    assert wd_a.status_code in (422, 423), "Withdrawal must be deferred while in HOLD"
    reg_b = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=payload_b, headers=auth_headers)
    assert reg_b.status_code == 201
    id_b = reg_b.json()["id"]
    cleanup_biller(id_b)
    headers_b = {**auth_headers, "X-Test-Clock": str(<utcnow_epoch>() + 86400)}
    status_b = <api_client>.get(f"/billers/{id_b}", headers=headers_b).json()["status"]
    assert status_b == "ACTIVE", f"Expected ACTIVE at T+86400, got {status_b}"
    wd_b = <api_client>.post("/withdrawals", json={"biller_id": id_b, "amount": "10.00"}, headers=headers_b)
    assert wd_b.status_code in (200, 201, 202), "Withdrawal must succeed after hold expires"

tests.name: test_biller_state_transitions_new_to_hold_to_active
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
No existing biller with the target account_number for this customer
actions: Advance clock to T+1 s → GET /billers/{id} → confirm still 'HOLD'
Advance clock to T+86400 s → GET /billers/{id} → confirm state transitions to 'ACTIVE'
Advance clock to T+86401 s → GET /billers/{id} → confirm remains 'ACTIVE'
expected: At T+86401 s: status == 'ACTIVE' (stable, no regression)
trace: R-FIN-048: 24-hour hold before biller becomes usable
R-FIN-048: state path NEW → HOLD → ACTIVE
tests.executable:
def test_biller_state_transitions_new_to_hold_to_active(auth_headers, unique_biller_payload, cleanup_biller):
    base_epoch = <utcnow_epoch>()
    resp = <api_client>.post("/billers", json=unique_biller_payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    biller_id = resp.json()["id"]
    cleanup_biller(biller_id)
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "HOLD"
    def get_status(offset):
        h = {**auth_headers, "X-Test-Clock": str(base_epoch + offset)}
        r = <api_client>.get(f"/billers/{biller_id}", headers=h)
        assert r.status_code == 200
        return r.json()["status"]
    assert get_status(1) == "HOLD", "1 s after creation must be HOLD"
    assert get_status(86400) == "ACTIVE", "At exactly 86400 s must be ACTIVE"
    assert get_status(86401) == "ACTIVE", "At 86401 s must remain ACTIVE"
